mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal ongoing labor market strength and aggregate demand growth, which raises real interest rate expectations and pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases for three consecutive months but the 10-year yield fails to rise over the next 20 trading days (rank IC < 0), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the 12-month change in CPI (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2%, it triggers expectations of higher inflation, leading market participants to demand higher term premiums on long-term bonds, thus raising 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPI 12-month change exceeds 2% but the 10-year yield does not increase within 20 trading days, or if the directional accuracy is less than 60% over 10 such events, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: With unemployment (UNRATE) elevated above 6% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) at the zero lower bound, the economy is in a liquidity trap where monetary policy is ineffective, and deflation fears dominate, causing long-term yields to decline over the next 60 trading days as investors seek safe assets.
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but the 10-year yield does not decline over the next 60 trading days (i.e., positive mean return), or if the signal fails to predict direction in more than 50% of such episodes, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), positive GDP growth (GDPC1) signals that the worst of the recession is over, reducing recession risk and the flight-to-safety premium, thereby lowering 10-year yields over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 rises but the 10-year yield also rises over the next 60 trading days in more than 40% of quarters when UNRATE > 6%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening residential investment and consumer demand, which raises recession fears and pushes investors into Treasuries, lowering 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 5% in a month but the 10-year yield does not fall within the next 20 trading days, or the average return is positive over such events, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) expands supply capacity and reduces upward pressure on prices, which lowers inflation expectations and thus leads to lower 10-year Treasury yields over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but the 10-year yield does not decrease over the next 20 trading days in at least 55% of months, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and quarterly growth in GDP (GDPC1) reinforces strong consumer demand, which stokes inflation fears and drives 10-year yields higher over the following 60 trading days as markets price in tighter monetary policy.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0 and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0
failure_condition: If both growth conditions are met but the 10-year yield fails to rise over the next 60 trading days in more than 60% of instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) from near-zero levels signals the beginning of a tightening cycle, raising short-term rate expectations and pushing up long-term yields over the next 5 trading days as portfolio adjustment occurs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25 basis points in one week
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by >25bp but the 10-year yield does not increase over the next 5 trading days, or if the average 5-day return is negative over such events, the hypothesis is rejected.
